summary: A focused stage decomp-matching cleanup in src/melee/gr/grcastle.c simplified grCastle_801CDFD8 by removing extra temporaries, directly using grCs_804D6970 fields, and assigning literals directly. The PR body says this moved the function from 99.84% to 100%, and the bot report records one new match for grCastle_801CDFD8.

key_files.role: Only changed file; contains grCastle_801CDFD8 cleanup that removed redundant locals and simplified assignments.

what_changed.text: In grCastle_801CDFD8, redundant locals such as params, params2, random_range, neg_one, zero, base_value, and final_value were removed.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed local declarations in grCastle_801CDFD8

what_changed.text: The random range is now stored in a single local named range from grCs_804D6970->xA and passed to HSD_Randi when nonzero.
Assignments to gp->gv.castle9 shorts now use direct expressions and literals: xD4 receives grCs_804D6970->x8 + rand_result, xDC/xDA/xD8 receive -1, and xD6 receives 0.
The loaded bot report records one new match for main/melee/gr/grcastle item grCastle_801CDFD8, changing from 99.84% before to 100.00% after.

smart_moves.text: Kept the cleanup narrow to one function and one source file while preserving typed Ground/GroundVars field access.
Replaced decompiler-shaped value carrier locals with direct field and literal assignments, reducing source noise while reaching the claimed match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of params2, neg_one, zero, base_value, final_value
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows grCastle_801CDFD8 at 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For small nonmatching residue, redundant temporaries that only carry globals, constants, or a final expression can be worth simplifying; in this PR, direct grCs_804D6970 field reads and literal assignments coincided with a 100% function match.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: redundant locals removed and direct assignments added
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report one new match from 99.84% to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Preserving typed nested field access such as gp->gv.castle9.* can coexist with match cleanup; this PR did not replace those fields with raw offsets.

naming_conventions.text: The local random range variable was renamed from random_range to the shorter range while its role remained locally obvious from grCs_804D6970->xA and HSD_Randi(range).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: s32 random_range replaced by s32 range

naming_conventions.text: No new semantic global or address-style symbol rename is evidenced; grCs_804D6970 and grCastle_801CDFD8 names were preserved.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: references to grCs_804D6970 and grCastle_801CDFD8 unchanged

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Local lifetime and expression-shape cleanup: remove intermediate locals and assign direct expressions/literals to influence or reflect MWCC codegen for the final 0.16% mismatch.
No evidence of pragmas, inline assembly, PAD_STACK, volatile steering, dummy storage, or data-section edits in this PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: one C function cleanup only

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code +124 bytes and one new match: main/melee/gr/grcastle grCastle_801CDFD8, +1 byte, 99.84% before and 100.00% after.

follow_up_queries: grCs_804D6970 xA x8 castle9 xD4 xDC xDA xD8 xD6
decompiler-shaped locals direct literal assignments MWCC match

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2544 identity, state MERGED, author, timestamps, changed file counts
human_pr_text.md: PR body states minor cleanup from 99.84% to 100%
raw_diff: complete one-hunk diff for src/melee/gr/grcastle.c
notes: No human review comments or review records are present in the loaded slice.
The bot report supports the match result, but the slice does not include full build logs or objdiff details beyond the report summary.
The PR does not explain why the exact source simplification matched, so matching tactics are described at the observable diff level rather than as author intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Simplify decompiler-shaped carrier locals when cleaning a near-match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2544, grCastle_801CDFD8 reached a reported 100.00% match after removing locals that only carried grCs_804D6970, constants, or final assignment values, replacing them with direct field reads and literal assignments.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report one new match 99.84% to 100.00%

accepted_candidate_records.title: Direct literal and expression assignments can resolve small function mismatches
accepted_candidate_records.text: The diff changed assignments through neg_one, zero, base_value, and final_value temporaries into direct assignments of -1, 0, and grCs_804D6970->x8 + rand_result, with the bot report showing grCastle_801CDFD8 as newly matched.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a global naming standard from random_range being shortened to range; the evidence is one local variable in one function.
Do not infer reviewer intent or human approval rationale; no review comments are present.
Do not generalize that all temporary locals should be removed; the evidence only supports this focused cleanup where the bot report showed a match.
